title: [$250] Toggling on "Enforce Custom Report Names" within Workspaces > Reports overwrites manual edits made to report titles

labels.name: Help Wanted

body:
**Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **Issue Reported by** : @brianlee-expensify **Slack Conversation**: [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1777660082789929) **Expected Result:** Manual edits made to report titles should not change back to the formula-generated report titles once "Enforce Custom Report Names" is toggled on **Actual Result:** Manual edits made to report titles are changing back to the formula-generated report titles once "Enforce Custom Report Names" is toggled on **Actions Performed:** 1. Go to Workspaces > Reports > Custom Report Names on Expensify Classic 2. Toggle on "Enforce Custom Report Names" with the formula field set to {report:submit:from} Expense Report {report:startdate} 3. Create a new report and make sure it follows that formula 4. Go back and toggle off "Enforce Custom Report Names" 5. Change the report title to something random 6. Go back and toggle on "Enforce Custom Report Names" 7. Refresh the report page to see the report title revert back to the formula-generated format MelvinBot analysis [here](https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/630935#issuecomment-4360783837) https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/52fb70cd-83bc-4846-b4e7-f78b2a6b635a https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ede7adc6-0477-408d-a041-72aa593fe129

title: [$250] [Quality] Generalize seed-full-reconnect helper across Onyx clear flows

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
This issue tracks the fix for duplicate \`ReconnectApp\` requests being fired in multiple Onyx clear flows beyond the delegate account switch. The same \`Onyx.clear\` → \`openApp\` race that was fixed in the delegate-account-switch path (#89262) also exists in three other flows that go through \`subscribeToFullReconnect\`: - \`src/libs/actions/Session/index.ts\` — supportal restore stashed session - \`src/libs/actions/App.ts\` (\`RESET_REQUIRED\` listener) — Troubleshoot/Reset Onyx - \`src/libs/actions/App.ts\` (\`clearOnyxAndResetApp\`) — exit imported state This PR extracts a generalized helper \`clearOnyxAndSeedFullReconnect(keysToPreserve, extraSeeds?)\` that always seeds \`LAST_FULL_RECONNECT_TIME=now\` atomically and clears with the preserve list + the seeded keys, preventing unnecessary \`ReconnectApp\` calls in all these flows. **PR:** #89980 --- cc @mountiny @TMisiukiewicz @abzokhattab Upwork Automation - Do Not Edit * Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022049849402501236092 * Upwork Job ID: 2049849402501236092 * Last Price Increase: 2026-05-08 Issue Owner Current Issue Owner: @abzokhattab
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab

title: [$250] Search should default to the type matching the current page context

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Problem When a user initiates a keyword search from the **Reports** page, the search defaults to `type:expense` and switches to the Expenses view. This is unexpected — if you're on the Reports page, a keyword search should search within reports (`type:expense-report`), not expenses. This is especially confusing when searching by report ID (e.g., `R00AbC12deFG`) from the Reports page. Instead of finding the matching report, it runs a keyword search for that string at the expense level. The user has to back out, return to Reports, and perform an explicit report ID search to find what they're looking for. ## Expected Behavior - Searching from the **Expenses** page should default to `type:expense` - Searching from the **Reports** page should default to `type:expense-report` - The search context should match whichever page/tab the user is currently viewing ## Additional Context There's also a related UX suggestion to make the search input above the table more visually connected to the table itself (acting as a keyword filter for the current view), while keeping the global search router separate with more visual presence. **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C03U7DCU4/p1778073400727669

title: [$250] Show 'Scanning' date and sort scanning receipts to top of expenses list

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
When a user scans a receipt from the expenses page, the newly scanning expense gets a default date of "00:00:00 UTC today", which causes it to appear buried beneath other expenses created later that day. Users expect the scanning receipt to appear at the top of the list so they can easily track it. ## Proposed Solution Show "Scanning" instead of the date for scanning receipts, and sort them to the top of the expenses list. No backend changes needed — the display and sort are purely frontend concerns. ### 1. Show "Scanning" instead of the date There's already a precedent — the merchant column does exactly this at [`TransactionItemRow/index.tsx:159-161`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/d166392bc802b639bed97d96343a279a3619ec6f/src/components/TransactionItemRow/index.tsx#L159-L161): ```ts if (isScanning(transactionItem) && shouldShowMerchant) { merchant = translate('iou.receiptStatusTitle'); } ``` Apply the same pattern for the date column at the two `DateCell` call sites: - **Wide layout:** [`TransactionItemRow/index.tsx:326`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/d166392bc802b639bed97d96343a279a3619ec6f/src/components/TransactionItemRow/index.tsx#L326) — pass `translate('iou.receiptStatusTitle')` instead of `createdAt` when `isScanning(transactionItem)` is true - **Narrow layout:** [`TransactionItemRow/index.tsx:736`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/d166392bc802b639bed97d96343a279a3619ec6f/src/components/TransactionItemRow/index.tsx#L736) — same change [`DateCell.tsx`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/d166392bc802b639bed97d96343a279a3619ec6f/src/components/Search/SearchList/ListItem/DateCell.tsx) would need a small tweak to skip date formatting when the value isn't a real date (or accept a `displayText` override prop). ### 2. Sort scanning receipts to top Add scanning-priority logic in the two sort locations, **before** the normal `compareValues` call: - **Search/expenses list:** [`SearchUIUtils.ts` (`getSortedTransactionData`)](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/d166392bc802b639bed97d96343a279a3619ec6f/src/libs/SearchUIUtils.ts#L3590) — inside the `.sort()` callback, check `isScanning(a)` / `isScanning(b)` first. If one is scanning and the other isn't, return the scanning one first (regardless of sort direction). - **Report transaction list:** [`MoneyRequestReportTransactionList.tsx:286`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/d166392bc802b639bed97d96343a279a3619ec6f/src/components/MoneyRequestReportView/MoneyRequestReportTransactionList.tsx#L286) — same wrapper logic around `compareValues`. ### 3. No backend changes needed The `created` date stays as today's date (no fake future dates). [`isScanning`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/d166392bc802b639bed97d96343a279a3619ec6f/src/libs/TransactionUtils/index.ts#L1403-L1415) already detects the scanning state from `receipt.state`, and once SmartScan completes, `isScanning` returns false and the expense reverts to normal date display + sort position automatically. --- **Parent issue:** https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/630432

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:**v9.3.68-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** No **Issue reported by:** SRAM **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#expensify-bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1778153926921989) ## Action Performed: - User has the `Auditor` role - User has reports to approve on a workspace that is past the billing grace period. 1. Click on `Approve` 2. Observe the NotFound page ## Expected Result: The user should see the restriction action page with the variation that is shown to `user`. ## Actual Result: The [code for this page](https://github.com/callstack-internal/Expensify-App/blob/b1ba0a22318f9b9f96ea5e284ac61ea5829fcf6b/src/pages/RestrictedAction/Workspace/WorkspaceRestrictedActionPage.tsx#L85-L100) only evaluates `Owner`, `Admin`, `User` roles. So a user with the `Auditor` role hits a 404 NotFound page. ## Workaround: Can the user still use Expensify without this being fixed? Have you informed them of the workaround? ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos In-line above.
